focus: Trace post-parse hook sources and identify which source can place a no-bash/no-powershell object into userPromptSubmitted execution

graphEvents.summary: Copilot 1.0.34 reads .claude/settings*.json and .github/copilot/settings*.json repo hooks, not only .github/hooks/*.json
sessionStart filters to type=command but userPromptSubmitted maps every entry, explaining the asymmetric failure
Real logs show the failure before model request setup, matching userPromptSubmitted hook timing
Session-store and ad-hoc SDK hooks add callback functions, not raw command objects

summary: Copilot 1.0.34 repo settings loader reads .claude/settings.json, .claude/settings.local.json, .github/copilot/settings.json, and .github/copilot/settings.local.json; its repo settings schema includes hooks.
loadAllHooks merges repo settings hooks into the same hook list as .github/hooks/*.json before loadHooks/Nct/ntr convert them into executable callbacks.
The active .claude/settings.local.json has a Claude-style UserPromptSubmit wrapper with nested hooks[0].command but no top-level bash, powershell, or command on the wrapper object.
Copilot aliases UserPromptSubmit to userPromptSubmitted and dZe preserves wrapper objects while adding _vsCodeCompat, allowing a Claude wrapper to enter the Copilot userPromptSubmitted path.

The asymmetry is source-backed: sessionStart filters entries to type=command before g2, but userPromptSubmitted maps every entry through g2; g2 passes the original entry to jer, which throws when bash and powershell are both absent.
wireSessionStoreTracking/CUn and createHooksProxy are not direct raw-object sources: they merge or add hook callback functions rather than command config objects.
A representative real Copilot log shows the missing-shell hook failure immediately before model request setup, matching userPromptSubmitted pre-provider timing.
A valid .github/hooks/*.json flat bash patch is insufficient if repo settings also provide Claude-style nested wrappers, because repo settings hooks are merged into the same effective hook list.
F25 explains why sessionStart can avoid the missing-shell error while userPromptSubmitted fails with the same Claude-style wrapper source.
F21 expands the config path answer to include .claude/settings*.json and .github/copilot/settings*.json repo settings.
F23 supplies the concrete active wrapper object that F25 shows can reach jer without bash or powershell.

approach: Treat wireSessionStoreTracking or ad-hoc SDK hook proxying as the direct source of the raw missing-shell command object
reason: Both paths contribute callback functions, while the failing executor requires a raw command config object lacking bash and powershell.
